title: Skyline Shift
prompt:
Create a modern 187-second Electro Pop song for a web radio / web TV broadcast.

Mood: Uplifting, Energetic, Optimistic.
Tempo: 120 BPM.
Style: Clean, radio-ready, modern pop production with a strong rhythmic drive.
Production: Polished, dynamic, wide stereo image, modern reverb and delay effects.
Instruments: Synthesizers (pads, arpeggiated sequences, leads), electronic drums (kick, snare, hi-hats), processed bass, layered vocal samples.

Structure:
0:00 - 0:14 intro: Gentle synth pads and a subtle arpeggio build anticipation.
0:14 - 0:52 verse/groove: Driving beat enters with a pulsating bassline and simple synth chords.
0:52 - 1:11 build/pre-chorus: Filter sweeps and increasing intensity on the drums create a build-up.
1:11 - 1:48 chorus/drop/main hook: Catchy, repetitive vocal hook ("Feel the rise") layered with a powerful synth lead and a driving beat.
1:48 - 2:25 second verse or soft bridge: Reduced instrumentation, more emphasis on the vocal hook and atmospheric pads.
2:25 - 2:59 final chorus/drop/main hook: Full instrumentation returns, the vocal hook is more prominent, with added layers and harmonies.
2:59 - 3:07 final chorus continues with smooth fade out.

Vocals: Short, catchy vocal hook (“Feel the rise”) repeated throughout the song.  Harmonized vocals in the final chorus.

Lyrics: (Not applicable, only vocal hook)

The final 8 seconds must fade out smoothly and naturally. No abrupt ending. No spoken outro.

Negative prompt: low quality, distorted vocals, out of tune vocals, bad timing, messy mix, muddy bass, harsh highs, old-fashioned arrangement, theatrical singing, opera vocals, excessive vibrato, abrupt ending, spoken outro, crackle, noise, clipping, overcompressed, random tempo changes, long silence.
